meaning: n.不妥协态度,不让步,不调和
examples: He often appeared angry and frustrated by the intransigence of both sides.
Political intransigence. How do we save the world?
So the "realists" are adapting to the reality of Iranian intransigence.
That intransigence puts it at odds with many of its peers around the world.
Google "may be using its potential spectrum acquisition as a hedge against carrier intransigence, " Schachter writes.
